title: EU Parliament Holds Hearing on Cross-Border AI Data Rules
body: The European Parliament's Committee on Civil Liberties convened a hearing Tuesday on the adequacy of existing frameworks governing AI companies that process European citizens' data outside the bloc. The session, chaired by MEP Florian Hartmann of Germany, featured testimony from regulators, academics, and one industry representative. The Vantara AI situation was raised repeatedly as a live example of enforcement gaps, though the company was not formally named in the hearing title. FTC Commissioner Angela Torres attended as an observer, part of an ongoing transatlantic dialogue on AI governance, and was seated in the gallery. She did not testify. DPC Deputy Commissioner Maeve Clancy testified that the commission does not comment on open inquiries but acknowledged general concerns about the adequacy of standard contractual clauses for AI systems that do continuous, real-time data processing. The hearing produced no binding outcomes but is expected to feed into a draft proposal on AI data transfer rules due later this spring.
